title: Implied Volatility Surface Construction
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Implied Volatility Surface Construction: Implied vol surface construction: build smooth, arbitrage-free implied volatility surface from sparse market quotes. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.interpolation.cubic_spline…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~30; sparse_data_interpolation_error, butterfly_arbitrage_violation set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: sigma_impl(k, T) modeled by SVI parameterization: w(k) = a + b*(rho*(k-m) + sqrt((k-m) ** 2 + sigma ** 2))
E.world_state_x: continuous implied vol surface sigma(K/F, T) satisfying calendar and butterfly arbitrage constraints
E.observation_y: sparse market implied vols sigma_i at (K_i, T_j) grid points

W.regime: Existence of the recovered implied_vol_surface is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 30); sparse_data_interpolation_error dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Market bid ask gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: interpolation RMSE < 20 bp with SVI fit. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~30. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |sparse_data_interpolation_error|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_slices * N_params_SVI) per calibration per iteration
